commands.name: commit
commands.description: Generate and apply a conventional commit
prompt: 1. Run `git diff --cached` to get staged changes.
2. If empty, ask the user to stage changes and stop.
- Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert
- Subject: imperative mood, lowercase, no period, max 50 chars
- Body (if needed): wrap at 72 chars, explain _why_ not _how_, one liner preferred
5. If multiple scopes, use best judgement.

commands.name: pr
prompt: 1. Run `git remote get-url origin` to identify the repo.
2. Run `git diff main...HEAD` for all changes on this branch.
3. Run `git log main...HEAD --oneline` for commit history.
5. Generate a PR (use template if found, else):

commands.name: standup
commands.description: Generate daily standup from git activity
prompt: 1. Run `git log --since="24 hours ago" --oneline --author="$(git config user.name)"`.
2. Run `git diff HEAD~5...HEAD --stat` for recent file changes.
- **Today**: what's next (infer from WIP or last commit)

commands.name: explain
commands.role: Senior Engineer explaining to a smart peer
commands.argsDefault: Explain the last code discussed in context.
prompt: - **How it works** — key logic, data flow
- **Why it exists** — the problem it solves
3. Keep tight. Code snippets only when they clarify.

commands.name: bugs
commands.description: Find bugs, logic errors, and edge cases
commands.role: Senior Engineer doing a bug review
commands.argsDefault: Review the last code discussed in context.
prompt: - Error handling gaps (swallowed errors, missing fallbacks)
- Security issues (injection, unvalidated input, exposed secrets)
3. For each bug: location, what, why, fix with code snippet.

commands.name: refactor
commands.description: Refactor code for clarity, performance, or maintainability
commands.role: Senior Engineer doing a focused refactor
commands.argsDefault: Refactor the last code discussed in context.
prompt: - Preserve exact behavior — no feature changes
- Minimal diff — don't rewrite what isn't broken

commands.name: review
commands.role: Senior Engineer doing a code review
prompt: 1. Run `git diff --cached` for staged changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` if nothing staged.
2. Review for: bugs, security, error handling, performance, naming, test gaps.
4. End with one-line verdict: approve / request changes / needs discussion.

commands.name: test
commands.description: Write tests for a file or function
commands.argsDefault: Write tests for the last code discussed in context.
prompt: 2. Identify the testing framework in use (jest, vitest, mocha, etc). Match it.
3. Write tests covering: happy path, edge cases (empty, null, boundary), error cases, async behavior.
4. One assertion per test where possible.
5. No mocking unless truly necessary. Prefer real behavior.
6. Write tests to the appropriate test file.
